title: Best Peer-to-Peer Fundraising Software for Nonprofits 2026
description: Explore top peer fundraising platforms including CrowdRise, TeamRaiser, and RallyBound. Leverage supporter networks for expanded donor reach and engagement.
content:
Best Peer-to-Peer Fundraising Software for Nonprofits 2026

Peer-to-peer fundraising has transformed nonprofit outreach by enabling supporters to create personal fundraising campaigns linked to organizational objectives. This approach leverages personal networks for expanded reach, tapping into social connections that organizations cannot access directly. Research indicates that personal recommendations significantly influence giving decisions, making network-based fundraising a powerful donor acquisition channel. In 2026, peer-to-peer fundraising platforms have evolved to address this opportunity through sophisticated tools that empower supporters while maintaining organizational coordination.

The Power of Network-Based Fundraising

Peer-to-peer fundraising operates on the principle that personal relationships drive giving behavior. When supporters ask friends, family, and colleagues to contribute to causes they champion, those requests carry authenticity and personal connection that organizational appeals cannot replicate. This personal dimension increases response rates and potentially attracts donors who would not respond to organizational outreach.

The network expansion effect multiplies organizational reach exponentially. Each supporter connects the organization to their personal network, potentially dozens or hundreds of contacts. When multiple supporters engage, the combined reach far exceeds direct organizational donor acquisition capacity. This multiplication makes peer-to-peer fundraising particularly valuable for organizations seeking to expand donor base beyond existing reach.

Social proof amplifies peer fundraising effectiveness. When donors see friends contributing to causes, they receive implicit endorsement that organizational messaging cannot provide. This social validation reduces hesitancy and increases willingness to contribute. Peer fundraising platforms leverage this social proof through features that display supporter fundraising activity and network contribution patterns.

Supporter engagement deepens through peer fundraising participation. When supporters champion causes through personal fundraising, they invest more deeply in organizational mission. This engagement creates stronger supporter relationships, potentially increasing long-term loyalty and engagement. Peer fundraising thus serves dual purposes: donor acquisition and supporter deepening.

Leading Peer-to-Peer Fundraising Platforms Reviewed

1. CrowdRise

CrowdRise has established itself as a leading peer-to-peer fundraising platform, combining personal fundraising tools with organizational campaign coordination. The platform enables supporters to create individual fundraising pages while organizations maintain overarching campaign management. This balance of supporter autonomy and organizational coordination addresses the core peer fundraising dynamic.

The supporter page creation tools enable individuals to establish personal fundraising pages quickly, with templates that link to organizational campaigns. Supporters can customize their pages with personal stories, images, and messaging while maintaining connection to organizational branding. The platform provides guidance for effective page creation, helping supporters optimize their personal appeals.

CrowdRise's social integration facilitates sharing across social networks, with optimized formats for different social platforms. Supporters can share their fundraising pages through integrated social tools, reaching personal networks through preferred social channels. The platform tracks social sharing, providing visibility into network-based promotion effectiveness. These social capabilities enable supporters to leverage their social presence for fundraising.

The campaign management module enables organizations to create overarching campaigns that coordinate supporter efforts. Organizations can define campaign objectives, set collective goals, and track aggregate progress. The platform provides visibility into supporter fundraising activity, enabling organizational coordination and encouragement. These management capabilities maintain organizational oversight while supporting supporter autonomy.

Leaderboard and progress features create competitive elements that motivate supporter fundraising. Supporters can see their progress relative to campaign goals and peer fundraisers. These visibility elements create social dynamics that encourage continued effort. Organizations can configure competitive features appropriately, balancing motivation with collaborative atmosphere.

CrowdRise offers pricing based on transaction volume, with platform fees applied to contributions. Organizations can evaluate fee structures against peer fundraising returns, considering both cost and benefit. Many organizations find that peer fundraising returns justify platform investment, as acquired donors may contribute repeatedly over time.

2. TeamRaiser

TeamRaiser provides peer-to-peer fundraising capabilities within the broader Blackbaud ecosystem, integrating with organizational donor management and financial systems. This integration enables peer fundraising data to flow into organizational records, creating unified donor tracking. Organizations using Blackbaud platforms may find TeamRaiser particularly suitable due to ecosystem integration.

The team creation features enable supporters to form fundraising teams, with collective goals and coordinated efforts. Team structures facilitate group fundraising, particularly for events such as walks, runs, and challenges. Supporters can join teams, contribute to team goals, and collaborate on fundraising strategies. These team capabilities support the group dynamics that characterize many peer fundraising activities.

TeamRaiser's event integration enables peer fundraising connected to organizational events, with tools for event registration, team formation, and fundraising coordination. Organizations can create event-based campaigns that combine participation with fundraising. The platform handles event logistics alongside fundraising administration, creating unified event-fundraising workflows.

The personal page tools enable supporters to create individual fundraising pages within team or campaign contexts. Supporters can customize pages with personal content while maintaining connection to team or campaign objectives. The platform provides templates and guidance, helping supporters create effective pages without design expertise.

Communication features enable organizations to coordinate with peer fundraisers, providing encouragement, guidance, and updates. Organizations can send targeted messages to fundraisers, helping them optimize efforts. The platform tracks communication history, ensuring consistent supporter engagement. These communication capabilities help organizations manage distributed fundraising activity.

TeamRaiser pricing reflects its position within the Blackbaud ecosystem, with options available to Blackbaud platform users. Organizations should evaluate TeamRaiser alongside ecosystem benefits, considering integration value as well as standalone capabilities.

3. RallyBound

RallyBound positions itself as a peer-to-peer fundraising platform designed for modern social engagement, with emphasis on mobile experience and social integration. The platform addresses contemporary supporter expectations for mobile-first, socially connected fundraising experiences. This modern focus makes RallyBound particularly suitable for organizations engaging younger supporters who prioritize mobile and social channels.

The mobile-first design ensures that peer fundraising experiences function optimally on smartphones, both for fundraisers and donors. Supporters can create and manage fundraising pages through mobile interfaces, accommodating on-the-go engagement. Donors can contribute through mobile-optimized giving processes, reducing friction for smartphone-based giving. These mobile capabilities address the substantial mobile traffic in contemporary fundraising.

RallyBound's social integration provides sophisticated sharing tools across major social platforms. Supporters can share fundraising pages through integrated social features, with optimized formats for each platform. The platform tracks social engagement, providing insights into sharing effectiveness. These social capabilities leverage supporter social presence for fundraising reach.

The page creation tools enable supporters to establish personal fundraising pages quickly, with templates and customization options. Supporters can add personal content, images, and stories while maintaining connection to organizational campaigns. The platform provides guidance for effective page creation, helping supporters optimize personal appeals. These creation tools balance supporter customization with organizational branding.

Campaign management features enable organizations to coordinate supporter fundraising through overarching campaign structures. Organizations can set collective goals, track aggregate progress, and communicate with fundraisers. The platform provides visibility into supporter activity, enabling organizational encouragement and coordination. These management capabilities maintain organizational oversight while supporting distributed fundraising.

RallyBound offers pricing based on transaction volume and feature requirements, with options accessible for organizations with moderate peer fundraising and scaling for sophisticated needs. The platform's modern focus particularly suits organizations engaging supporters through contemporary digital channels.

Peer-to-Peer Platform Comparison Overview

CapabilityCrowdRiseTeamRaiserRallyBoundBest Application
Team FeaturesGoodExcellentGoodTeamRaiser: Group fundraising
Event IntegrationGoodExcellentGoodTeamRaiser: Event campaigns

Page CreationExcellentGoodExcellentCrowdRise/RallyBound: Easy creation
CRM IntegrationGoodExcellentGoodTeamRaiser: Blackbaud ecosystem

Modern DesignGoodModerateExcellentRallyBound: Contemporary UX

Peer Fundraising Implementation Strategy

Effective peer fundraising requires strategic implementation beyond platform selection. Organizations should identify supporters likely to engage as peer fundraisers, targeting outreach to those with network reach and cause commitment. This targeting improves recruitment efficiency, engaging supporters who can actually generate peer fundraising returns.

Supporter preparation and training helps fundraisers optimize their efforts. Organizations should provide guidance on effective fundraising strategies, page creation, and network outreach. Training resources, templates, and ongoing support help fundraisers succeed. Organizations that invest in supporter preparation typically see better peer fundraising results than those that simply enable platform access.

Campaign structure influences peer fundraising effectiveness. Organizations should define clear campaign objectives, timeframes, and goals that provide context for supporter fundraising. Campaign messaging, impact stories, and progress updates help fundraisers communicate compellingly. Well-designed campaigns provide framework that enables successful supporter fundraising.

Future Trends in Peer-to-Peer Fundraising

Peer-to-peer fundraising technology continues to evolve, adapting to changing social dynamics and supporter expectations. Social commerce integration is advancing, with platforms enabling fundraising within social commerce contexts such as birthday fundraisers, milestone celebrations, and social occasions. These integrated approaches reduce friction by embedding fundraising into existing social activities.

Video content integration is gaining prominence, with peer fundraising platforms enabling video storytelling. Supporters can create video content that personalizes appeals more compellingly than text and images alone. Platforms are developing video creation and sharing tools that enable supporters to produce effective video content without production expertise.

Artificial intelligence is beginning to enhance peer fundraising, with AI-powered features for fundraiser matching, content recommendation, and success prediction. AI can identify supporters likely to succeed as fundraisers, recommend effective strategies, and predict fundraising outcomes. These capabilities help organizations optimize peer fundraising recruitment and support.

Integration with broader organizational systems is deepening, with peer fundraising data flowing into donor management, financial tracking, and communication systems. These integrations create unified organizational visibility, enabling coordinated engagement with donors acquired through peer channels. Connected systems transform peer fundraising from isolated activity into integrated donor acquisition channel.

As nonprofits evaluate peer-to-peer fundraising options in 2026, the potential for donor network expansion is substantial. By selecting platforms that support effective supporter fundraising and implementing thoughtfully, organizations can leverage supporter networks for significant donor acquisition and engagement deepening.
author: Social Fundraising Specialist
